page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz, supported by 16.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 2.0 GB of memory per core. The CPU architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512F, AVX-512 VNNI, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute workloads. The instance achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1850.49 and a multi-core score of 7748.54, alongside a Geekbench multi-core score of 5294.0. While cached memory read bandwidth is average at 418.63 GB/sec, uncached memory operations represent a performance bottleneck. Uncached large-block memory reads of 21.36 GB/sec and writes of 23.88 GB/sec fall into the poor and weak categories respectively, which is reflected in a weak PassMark memory score of 1773.28. Additionally, while the instance handles small 135M LLM models adequately, performance drops significantly on larger workloads, with 7B model prompt processing and text generation rated as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7.2xlarge offers dedicated CPU resources with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a resource-dense option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. It is appropriate for workloads such as software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations. However, the lack of local storage and the weak uncached memory bandwidth make it less optimal for memory-intensive databases or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B physical cores running at 2.7 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show average performance in CPU-heavy tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7748.54 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5294.0. However, uncached memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with large-block reads and writes falling into the poor and weak categories respectively. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads with low memory requirements. It is suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and moderate database operations, but is constrained by its memory bandwidth for larger data-intensive tasks.

tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized instance featuring a balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio for processing-heavy workloads without local storage requirements.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM hypervisor virtualization
Average CPU performance with AVX-512 instruction set support
Weak uncached memory bandwidth and no bundled local storage
